title: Intel 8237 Programmable DMA Controller
examTip: 8237 manages 4 independent DMA channels. Priority modes: Fixed (Channel 0 highest) or Rotating (round-robin). Transfer modes: Single, Block, Demand, Cascade. DREQ=DMA Request from device, DACK=DMA Acknowledge, HRQ=Hold Request to CPU, HLDA=Hold Acknowledge from CPU.
theory:
The Intel 8237 is a Programmable Direct Memory Access (DMA) controller designed to optimize data transfer speeds between high-speed peripheral devices and system memory. In standard transfer methods, the CPU reads data from a peripheral and writes it to memory, taking multiple bus cycles. The 8237 bypasses the CPU completely, allowing the peripheral to write directly to or read directly from memory in a single bus cycle.

Structure and Channels:
- Four Independent Channels: The 8237 provides 4 DMA channels, enabling up to 4 peripherals to interface with the DMA controller. Each channel has a 16-bit Current Address Register, Current Word Count Register, Base Address Register, and Base Word Count Register.
- Priority Resolver: Resolves conflicting requests from peripherals. It can operate in Fixed Priority mode (where Channel 0 has the highest priority and Channel 3 the lowest) or Rotating Priority mode (where priority rotates round-robin so no channel is starved).
- Read/Write and Timing Logic: Manages bus control. When a DMA channel is activated, the 8237 sends a Hold Request (HRQ) to the CPU. The CPU releases control of the address, data, and control buses and replies with a Hold Acknowledge (HLDA). The 8237 then takes over the system bus to perform the transfer.

Modes of Operation:
- Single Transfer Mode: Transfers one byte of data per DMA cycle. The bus control is then released back to the CPU. This is the most CPU-friendly mode as it doesn't hog the bus.
- Block Transfer Mode: Transfers data blocks continuously until the Word Count register reaches zero (Terminal Count) or an external EOP signal is received.
- Demand Transfer Mode: Data is transferred continuously as long as the requesting device maintains its DREQ line active. If the device deactivates DREQ, the DMA transfer is suspended until it is reactivated.
- Cascade Mode: Allows multiple 8237 chips to be connected together (cascaded) to expand the number of DMA channels beyond 4. Under this mode, one channel's DREQ/DACK is connected to another 8237's HRQ/HLDA.
keyPoints: Four independent programmable DMA channels with 16-bit address and word count registers
Uses HRQ and HLDA signals to perform bus arbitration and take master control from the CPU
Supports 4 transfer modes: Single, Block, Demand, and Cascade
Priority resolution can be set to Fixed (0 > 1 > 2 > 3) or Rotating (round-robin)
Includes active-low control inputs/outputs: IOR̄, IOW̄, MEMR̄, MEMW̄ for direct memory-to-device routing
EOP̄ (End of Process) pin acts as a bidirectional signal indicating or triggering terminal count completion
useCases: Legacy PC architecture floppy disk controller and sound card data streaming
High-speed data transfers between network interface cards and system RAM
Hard disk drive buffer reads in early microcomputer designs
